communication_style.input_method: Often voice-dictating while walking or in noisy environments
preferences: Show me it works, then we can make it pretty
Lateral thinking and layered jokes appreciated
Will tell you your architecture is shit if it is
dislikes: Enterprise theater (unnecessary process for process sake)
Having to repeat context across AI sessions

working_style.approach: Spitball ideas conversationally first, then build fast
working_style.tools: Mac Mini running Claude Code, Node.js/TypeScript stack
working_style.methodology: Uses GSD (Get Shit Done) framework for Claude Code projects
working_style.autonomy: Works best with high autonomy, pushes back on bad decisions
strengths: Deep domain knowledge across multiple industries
Naming things (Baked Potato, Breakfast Club — always multi-layered)

hospitality_tech.details: 20+ years. POS systems (Square, RedCat), kitchen display systems, menu engineering, inventory management, staff scheduling, delivery platform integration (UberEats, DoorDash). Built complete hospitality intelligence platform (Cafe Brain).

systems_integration.details: Core professional identity. Connecting legacy systems to modern stacks, protocol translation, data migration, API orchestration. The person you call when System A needs to talk to System B and nobody built an interface.

marine_electronics.details: NMEA 2000/0183, Signal K, OpenCPN, vessel management systems. Building Kees — an AI orchestration layer for superyacht management. MoCA 2.5 and G.hn networking for vessel infrastructure.

iot_industrial_control.details: Home Assistant, ESPHome, ESP32, Tapo/TP-Link ecosystem. Industrial control and monitoring. DIY solar (23 panels, Fronius inverter), automated salt chlorination.

azure_infrastructure.details: Azure AD, Conditional Access, break-glass accounts, security hardening. Manages Azure environments for consulting clients.

blockchain.details: Hands-on experience running Bittensor nodes, mining TAO. Understands validator/miner economics. Early adopter energy.

active.name: Breakfast Club
active.description: Self-sovereign AI identity system. Git-based storage, MongoDB hot cache, MCP server, transparency log.

active.name: Baked Potato POS
active.description: Custom hospitality POS with radial modifier UI, ECS-inspired entity model, audio order pipeline (Whisper), E-stop feedback system. North star: 'The customer orders a rare steak for $30. They get a rare steak. It costs $30.'

active.name: Moneypenny Partnership
active.description: Profit-share partnership with Moneypenny bar/restaurant owner Yam Adhikari. Nick is owner/operator. Rebranding strategy (MP's day, Q evening), happy hour, morning coffee window, menu rationalization from 60+ to ~30 items.

active.name: Kees
active.description: AI orchestration layer for superyacht management. Signal K as vessel data broker, OpenCPN for navigation, Node-RED for automation, browser-based airshow display, 3D boat model via photogrammetry.

active.name: Nick Bot
active.description: Personal Telegram bot bridging Telegram to Claude Code on Mac Mini. Session management, agent spawning, cron scheduling, memory injection.

completed.name: Cafe Brain
completed.description: Comprehensive Node.js hospitality intelligence platform for partner Lou's café. Square POS integration, TimescaleDB, MongoDB, AIAnalyticsEngine, weather API, Kitchen Display System. Voice assistant spec using Jetson Orin Nano.

completed.name: Moneypenny POS Migration
completed.description: Migrated from legacy RedCat POS (Windows XP/Firebird 2.5) to Square. CSV categorization of 314 products. Square MCP integration testing.

completed.name: Azure Security Hardening
completed.description: Azure infrastructure hardening for client John's environment after crypto mining incident. Break-glass account setup, Conditional Access policies.

completed.name: DIY Solar Installation
completed.description: 23 LG NeON 2 355W panels, Fronius Symo 8.2-3-M inverter. Self-designed and installed.

personal.partner: Lou (previously ran Mutha Focaccia café on TAFE NSW property)

professional: Open source and open standards over proprietary lock-in
People should own their data and their AI relationships
Technology should serve the user, not extract from them
Honesty over diplomacy — but not cruel about it
technical_opinions: AI is going to be the operating system of the future — device/OS won't matter
The relationship between a person and their AI is as valuable as any other data they own
Most enterprise software exists to justify its own existence
If a system requires a 50-page manual, the system is wrong
Microservices are usually over-engineering for the scale people actually operate at
